feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Rochdale sign former MK Dons defender Maguire
entry.content: Rochdale sign former MK Dons defender Laurence Maguire on a two-year deal.

entry.title: Ole Miss football lands commitment from Colton Johnson, 2027 TE from Upperman
entry.content: Upperman's three-star tight end Colton Johnson has committed to an SEC school.

entry.title: Ole Miss football lands commitment from Colton Johnson, 2027 TE from Upperman
entry.content: Upperman's three-star tight end Colton Johnson has committed to an SEC school.

entry.title: T.J. Hughes’ leadership and skill could find him on an opening-night roster
entry.content: The Colorado Avalanche weren’t the only club in the organization to make a playoff run this season; the Colorado Eagles also made their own push to the conference finals, and T.J. Hughes’ clutch gene and scoring ability shone throughout their success. Hughes’ success with the Eagles has already excited pundits about how camp will go […]

entry.title: Pato O'Ward in 'one of the luckiest positions' after Arrow McLaren driver changes, blasts critics
entry.content: Popular IndyCar driver Pato O'Ward spoke Wednesday in Milwaukee about his new teammates, and supported Scott Dixon through "disrespect that he's received."

entry.title: Live Updates from the World Cup quarterfinal France vs. Morocco
entry.content: Keep up with all the action at today's final World Cup match from Boston Stadium between France and Morocco.

entry.title: Barcelona’s €20m bid for Karim Adeyemi rejected by Borussia Dortmund
entry.content: Barcelona have seen an initial €20 million bid for Karim Adeyemi rejected by Borussia Dortmund, according to Marca. The Catalan giants have made the surprise move for the Germany star but will have to raise their offer if they are to bring him to Camp Nou. Marca say that Dortmund want at least the €33m […]

entry.title: What time is France vs. Morocco? Live stream, TV channel, kickoff schedule for World Cup 2026 quarterfinal match
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch the France vs. Morocco World Cup match, including where to watch on TV or live stream, what time the game starts and more.

entry.title: Fed's Williams expects energy prices to abate even as Iran war flares
entry.content: Fed's Williams expects energy prices to abate even as Iran war flares

entry.title: BofA Raises its Price Target on Amazon.com (AMZN)
entry.content: BofA Raises its Price Target on Amazon.com (AMZN)

entry.title: Indiana Fever vs. Phoenix Mercury: How to watch Caitlin Clark's next game tonight, time, channel, and more
entry.content: How to watch Caitlin Clark and the Indiana Fever play the Phoenix Mercury this Thursday.

entry.title: FIFA lashes 'unfounded allegations' after Argentina-Egypt clash
entry.content: FIFA referees chief Pierluigi Collina has hit out at "unfounded allegations" against World Cup match officials in the wake of Argentina's controversial last 16 victory over Egypt."Nobody can question the integrity of the FIFA World Cup match officials.

entry.title: Eagles’ roster depth may separate them from NFL contenders
entry.content: The Eagles' roster features plenty of star power, but its greatest strength may be the quality of the players behind them.

entry.title: France vs. Morocco (live updates): Quarterfinal showdown marks World Cup finale for Boston Stadium
entry.content: Four years after France crushed Morocco's World Cup dream, the two powerhouses meet again.

entry.title: Red Sox Place $130 Million All-Star Pitcher On Injured List
entry.content: He's out with a left groin strain

entry.title: Jordan Clarkson returning to Knicks on one-year minimum contract
entry.content: The Knicks are trying to keep the band together for another run.

entry.title: Kentucky officially misses on European star Nikola Kusturica
entry.content: The Kentucky Wildcats were once the favorites to land European wing Nikola Kusturica, but he has now committed elsewhere.

entry.title: Oxford placed under summer transfer embargo by EFL
entry.content: Oxford United are placed under a temporary transfer embargo by the English Football League.

entry.title: Can creditors put a lien on inherited property if you have unpaid debt?
entry.content: Have you inherited real estate while carrying debt? Here's what your creditors can and can't do to collect on it.

entry.title: Olympian David Hearn pleads not guilty to charges of damaging Reflecting Pool
entry.content: Three-time Olympian David Hearn was indicted July 2, after he was accused of ripping out a portion of the sealant from the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19.

entry.title: Spain says Trump softened rhetoric after learning of Madrid's contributions to NATO
entry.content: Spain says Trump softened rhetoric after learning of Madrid's contributions to NATO

entry.title: Israeli strike kills aid worker who brought World Cup screenings to Gaza
entry.content: An Israeli strike has killed an aid worker who organized World Cup game screenings for Palestinians in the war-torn Gaza Strip, a hospital director says.

entry.title: Police investigating after missing teen Nolan Wells found dead on Mississippi island
entry.content: Nolan Wells was found dead on Horn Island in Mississippi after the teen vanished during a Fourth of July trip with friends. CBS News' Skyler Henry has the latest details.

entry.title: Iran ramps up Strait of Hormuz threats amid renewed U.S. tensions
entry.content: Iran renewed its threats to close the Strait of Hormuz as tensions re-escalated following President Trump's announcement that the U.S.-Iran ceasefire ended. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

entry.title: Sony's RX10 V superzoom finally arrives with a new design and 4K 120p video
entry.content: Sony has finally unveiled the RX10 V superzoom with a 24-600mm zoom lens and 20.1-megapixel 1-inch sensor.

entry.title: Legionnaires’ outbreak rocks New York as experts warn of rising climate threat
entry.content: Officials say climate crisis ‘worsening our exposure’ to bacteria as at least 28 people sickened in ManhattanA New York outbreak of legionnaires’ disease, a rare but severe form of pneumonia, highlights the microbe’s growing and disproportionate impacts in a warming climate.At least 28 people have been sickened in an outbreak on the Upper East Side, a wealthy neighborhood between Central Park and the East River in Manhattan. Health department officials, seeking to stop the outbreak, have sampled water from nearly 160 building cooling towers to test for the bacteria. Continue reading...

entry.title: Claude's new Reflect dashboard wants to help you log off of Claude
entry.content: The Claude Reflect dashboard is like a mix of Spotify Wrapped and the screen time tool on your phone.

entry.title: New York is the first US state to ban smart glasses in all its courthouses
entry.content: New York has banned smart glasses in all 1,240 state, county, city, town and village courts.

entry.title: Here's how to block Meta from using your Instagram pictures for its AI
entry.content: Meta has automatically opted all public accounts into its Muse Image AI.

entry.title: Character.AI enters the microdrama arena with its own productions, but there’s a twist
entry.content: In an interesting twist that takes advantage of the company's core product, users can chat with these shows' characters, ask them questions, and even roleplay different storylines.

entry.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
entry.content: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

entry.title: Alienware 34 QD-OLED review: An ultrawide showcase for Samsung's latest display tech
entry.content: Alienware's latest 34-inch ultrawide gaming monitor is far brighter than before, and it can finally handle text without fringing.

entry.title: MPs urge Labour to ditch £330m Palantir software contract with NHS
entry.content: Cross-party group backs call from science and technology committee to look at alternative options, citing ‘serious mistrust’A second parliamentary committee has urged Labour to scrap Palantir’s £330m contract with the NHS, increasing pressure on the next prime minister over government deals with the US tech company.MPs on the health and social care select committee want the NHS to cut ties with Palantir and find a replacement for its system, which is supposed to unify and analyse huge amounts of often highly sensitive NHS health data. Continue reading...

entry.title: Nandan Nilekani leaves GP role at Fundamentum as it launches $200M third fund
entry.content: Nilekani remains Fundamentum's anchor investor as the firm expands its leadership team and targets AI and fintech startups in India.

entry.title: I quite fancy a Slate EV in a Crayola crayon color
entry.content: Slate's barebones EV trucks lack whimsy, which is why the company has teamed up with Crayola.

entry.title: SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5, its first built with Cursor's help
entry.content: SpaceXAI has rolled out Grok 4.5, the first model it trained with Cursor.

entry.title: Florida Preacher to Buy Abandoned Vermont College Campus
entry.content: The owner of defunct Green Mountain College intends to sell the property after his own ambitious development plans sputtered.

entry.title: France vs. Morocco projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup quarterfinals game in Boston
entry.content: France and Morocco meet at Gillette Stadium for a place in the World Cup semifinals. Here's the latest team news and projected starting lineups for the quarterfinal clash.

entry.title: The BBC and Channel 4 are in talks to merge streaming services... yet again
entry.content: The BBC wanted to launch a UK-wide streaming service with ITV and Channel 4 in 2007... and again in 2017... will the third time be the charm?

entry.title: What is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?
entry.content: Illness surging in Michigan and other US states is rarely life-threatening, CDC says – but it can have severe effectsCases of cyclosporiasis – a parasitic illness that can cause “explosive”, watery diarrhea – have surged across the United States in recent days, health officials have said, with an abnormally large outbreak of almost 1,000 cases reported in Michigan.Michigan typically reports about 50 cases a year, making the current outbreak the largest in the state’s history and one of the nation’s biggest in recent years. Ohio has also reported a sharp increase, with 177 cases as of 2 July, since the CDC’s last count. Continue reading...

entry.title: Buc-ee’s Is on a Rampage and No Mascot Is Safe
entry.content: The rest-stop chain is suing rivals over its trademarked beaver. Defenders of Mickey the Moose are having none of it.

entry.title: NHTSA calls out autonomous cars for interfering with first responders
entry.content: The NHTSA says it identified a 'pattern of driverless AVs' interfering with first responders. It's now demanding a solution from AV makers.

entry.title: Where to watch France vs. Morocco live stream, TV channel, start time for World Cup quarterfinal match
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know to watch the France vs. Morocco World Cup match, including where to watch on TV or live stream, what time the game starts and more.

entry.title: Argentina vs. Switzerland tickets: Best prices, seats and more for FIFA World Cup quarterfinals match
entry.content: Argentina and Switzerland are set to face off in the World Cup quarterfinals. Here's how to get tickets to the match in Kansas City.

entry.title: Where to watch Mercury vs. Fever today: Channel, time, TV schedule, live stream for Caitlin Clark WNBA game
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about Mercury vs. Fever today, including TV channel and streaming options for the WNBA matchup.

entry.title: World Cup prize money breakdown: How much teams, players win for each round in 2026 purse
entry.content: Here's the rundown on how much each team earned during their 2026 World Cup campaign.

entry.title: Why Morocco fans whistle during World Cup games: The story behind loud hiss you hear on TV
entry.content: When listening to Morocco play in the World Cup, you may notice some loud whistling.

entry.title: Phillies QB trade rumors should lead to deal with New York Jets
entry.content: It makes sense.

entry.title: Phillies and Blue Jays expected to fight for recent 37 home run slugger
entry.content: Both teams could use him.

entry.title: Jayson Tatum is pleased to see Jalen Brunson and OG Anunoby finally win for the Knicks
entry.content: Jayson Tatum is a fierce competitor, but he is happy that some of his friends won the championship this season.

entry.title: White Sox offense takes another night off in 5-0 loss
entry.content: Chicago manages just four singles, while Davis Martin's rough outing snaps the home series winning streak

entry.title: LeBron James’ Wife Savannah Calls Out Critics, Saying ‘People Have Definitely Made Careers’ of Talking About the Former Lakers Star
entry.content: Savannah opened up about her husband’s shocking decision to leave the Los Angeles Lakers in an episode of her podcast ‘Everybody’s Crazy’

entry.title: ‘My Survival Is On The Line’ – Shozo Isojima Promises Dominant Finish Against Eduard Folayang At The Inner Circle 21
entry.content: The Japanese star meets the former lightweight MMA king at Lumpinee Stadium on Friday.

entry.title: Tracy Cortez looking to make statement at UFC 329
entry.content: A women's flyweight bout between Top 10 ranked contenders Tracy Cortez (12-3) and Wang Cong (9-1) is the featured fight on Saturday's UFC 329 early preliminary fight card. Cortez will enter the fight looking to rebound from a submission loss to Erin Blanchfield in her last outing in November. ...

entry.title: Jacksonville State Football Preview 2026: Can the Gamecocks Get Back to the CUSA Championship?
entry.content: After three straight great years as an FBS program, Jacksonville State will be in for another big season if everything breaks right.

entry.title: Why USMNT will share half of $12.8 million World Cup payout with women’s team
entry.content: The USMNT’s woes continue even after their embarrassing elimination from the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Belgium gave the USMNT a thrashing during their Round of 16 battle, with a 4-1 win over the hosts, as it exposed some major flaws in their current squad.

entry.title: Truecaller clashes with India’s telecom regulator over anti-spam rules
entry.content: The caller ID company says users are increasingly ignoring and blocking calls from India's dedicated business number series.

entry.title: Judge orders release of $5.8 million payment Trump owes E. Jean Carroll
entry.content: A federal judge ruled that E. Jean Carroll should immediately receive the $5.8 million awarded to her after a jury found President Donald Trump liable of sexually abusing her in 1996 and later defaming her. NBC News legal analyst Danny Cevallos explains the ruling.

entry.title: Inside Trump’s Oval Office Decision to Ditch the Iran Ceasefire
entry.content: ‘I think it’s over,’ Trump said of the U.S. ceasefire with Iran two weeks after signing the interim peace plan.

entry.title: Platner says he will suspend his Senate campaign in Maine
entry.content: In a video posted on X, Graham Platner announced he is ending his Senate campaign after a woman accused him of sexual assault in 2021. Platner denied the allegation as “false” but said that he was suspending campaign operations. NBC News’ Ryan Nobles reports.

entry.title: Judge Orders Trump to Pay E. Jean Carroll $5 Million Damage Award
entry.content: The decision comes after the Supreme Court declined to take up the president’s appeal of the defamation and sexual-abuse verdict.

entry.title: NYC High-Rise Scare Is Harsh Reality Check for Office-to-Apartment Conversion Boom
entry.content: After the former Pfizer headquarters faced the risk of partial collapse, developers expect costlier safety reviews and insurance.

entry.title: Europe’s Embattled Populists Say They Answer Only to the Court of Public Opinion
entry.content: Marine Le Pen and Nigel Farage both stare down authorities, saying the voters will decide their fates.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
I hear and I forget. I see and I remember. I do and I understand.
Lucky Numbers: 29, 24, 6, 87, 17, 48

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use an old sun lotion bottle to store valuables in your beach bag.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: 14% of all facts and statistics are made up and 27% of people know that fact.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Bob Dylan
entry.content: “When you've got nothing, you've got nothing to lose.” — Bob Dylan

entry.title: U.S. Launches New Strikes on Iran After Trump Declares Ceasefire Over
entry.content: The president had earlier promised a second night of attacks and warned the strikes will get worse if Tehran continues to target commercial shipping.

entry.title: Prosecutors play video in court of suspect in Charlie Kirk's shooting after he turned himself in
entry.content: Prosecutors played a video clip in court Wednesday of suspect Tyler Robinson after he had turned himself in to Utah authorities a day after the shooting of conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

entry.title: Volkswagen is employing 100 sheep at a solar farm in Poland
entry.content: It's more of a baa-ter arrangement.

entry.title: Despite ‘misgivings,’ judge approves Elon Musk’s $1.5M SEC settlement
entry.content: The saga of Musk's tussle with the SEC over how he disclosed his growing stake in Twitter (now X) has come to an end.

entry.title: Wall Street’s Peace Trade Collides With Renewed Iran Fighting
entry.content: The Dow fell as global oil prices posted their steepest climb since May.

entry.title: Justin Bieber added to crammed World Cup final halftime show with BTS, Madonna and more
entry.content: The World Cup final halftime show better have a huge stage. Justin Bieber has been added as a co-headliner to a lineup that already includes BTS, Madonna and Shakira.

entry.title: Trump declares Iran ceasefire 'over' as U.S. launches new retaliatory strikes
entry.content: The latest U.S. strikes came after Iran targeted unarmed commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump saying he is “not sure” he wants to make a deal with the Iranians. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

entry.title: Tom Holland (not that one) battles with Elon Musk over ‘Odyssey’
entry.content: A historian who shares the famous actor’s name has waded into the discourse over the Christopher Nolan film on X, where Musk has been among the right-wing critics.

entry.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
entry.content: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

entry.title: Specialty farmers adapt harvests, protect crops in face of extreme heat
entry.content: Even as the sun started to set, the day's heat was still hanging in the air as Annie Woods walked back out to harvest squash and zucchini on her 50-acre farm.

entry.title: John Deere owners will get the right to repair their own equipment under a new FTC settlement
entry.content: It looks like John Deere owners can soon feel free to fix their own machines.

entry.title: Jewish Democrats Eyeing 2028 Presidential Bids Confront Party’s Hostility for Israel
entry.content: Rahm Emanuel delivered a tough-love message in Israel that has implications for his party’s politics back home.

entry.title: Feds demand autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders
entry.content: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said emergency scenes are not "edge cases."

entry.title: Canada's Carney visits Saudi Arabia as the prime minister seeks to expand ties with kingdom
entry.content: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney arrived in Saudi Arabia on Wednesday - the first visit to the kingdom by a Canadian leader in 26 years - as he seeks to expand his country's economic ties beyond its heavy reliance on the United States.

entry.title: Second person in 4 days is fatally shot in Memphis by federal task force member
entry.content: A member of a federal crime-fighting task force in Memphis shot and killed a person there on Wednesday, the second fatal shooting by a task force member in four days.

entry.title: Trump Backs Ukrainian Strikes Deep Inside Russia
entry.content: Plus, Iran faces a second day of U.S. strikes, and a wall built to protect a golf course causes an uproar.

entry.title: Man banned from national parks in Arizona after living illegally for years in Tonto National Forest
entry.content: A man pleaded guilty to illegally living in Tonto National Forest in Arizona, having spent eight years at unauthorized campsites and piled up heaps of trash in the nature preserve.

entry.title: Sepideh Moafi of 'The Pitt' is 'still in disbelief' about her first-ever Emmy nod
entry.content: The actor earned her first-ever Emmy nomination for her turn as Dr. Baran Al-Hashimi in the second season of HBO Max's popular medical drama.

entry.title: A Mother Blamed Vaccines for Her Twins’ Deaths. She Is Now Charged With Murder.
entry.content: Prosecutors in Payette County, Idaho, allege Andrea Renee Shaw suffocated her 18-month-old toddlers.

entry.title: Inflation Fears Retake the Stage
entry.content: Plus, diesel prices and AI politics

entry.title: The Trump administration is ramping up pressure on states to change election practices
entry.content: President Donald Trump's administration is threatening to withhold some federal funding from states that don't make changes to voting practices and is warning state election officials that they face arrest if they don't remove noncitizens from voter rolls.

entry.title: What to know about protecting pets from the New World screwworm fly
entry.content: Two New World screwworm cases in dogs are among more than 30 confirmed instances in Texas and New Mexico, prompting warnings Wednesday from veterinarians and humane societies that pet owners need to remain vigilant to protect their animals.

entry.title: Family demands an independent probe after ICE officer fatally shoots a man in Houston
entry.content: A Mexican national fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer in Houston had no criminal convictions during his decades living in the U.S. and was driving a crew to a homebuilding site when he was killed, his family and a Texas congresswoman said Wednesday.

entry.title: Fed officials were split on direction of interest rates at last meeting, minutes show
entry.content: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released minutes from its June 16-17 meeting.

entry.title: Trump unexpectedly switches back to old Air Force One, sidelining Qatari jet
entry.content: Trump unexpectedly switches back to old Air Force One, sidelining Qatari jet

entry.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal until 2027 after latest setback, Kalshi traders predict
entry.content: Speculators now see just a 43% chance that traffic flows return to normal by Dec. 1.

entry.title: Two teens detained in California after drinking, shooting toy guns out of Waymo robotaxi
entry.content: Waymo halted one of its robotaxis mid-ride and called California cops after it caught two teen passengers drinking booze and shooting pellets from toy guns, police said.

entry.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
entry.content: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Former Wisconsin judge spared prison for obstructing ICE arrest of Mexican immigrant
entry.content: Former Wisconsin Judge Hannah Dugan was spared from prison Wednesday for ushering a Mexican defendant out of her courtroom to evade U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents. A federal judge fined her $5,000 and cited her otherwise law-abiding life in issuing the sentence.

entry.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
entry.content: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

entry.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
entry.content: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

entry.title: Judge orders release of more than $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse, defamation case
entry.content: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

entry.title: Activists push California schools to teach 'Black English'
entry.content: A Black activist group is urging the California public school system to accommodate "Black English" in schools, arguing that Black children's use of the dialect must be "honored."
